title: cola tessmannii
intro: Cola tessmannii, also known as the West African cola nut tree, is a plant species that belongs to the family Sterculiaceae. Native to the tropical regions of West Africa, its seeds contain a rich source of caffeine, making it a valued plant for its medicinal and stimulant properties. The seeds have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, headache, and digestive issues, offering various health benefits such as improved circulation and immune system support. The seeds can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, which are commonly used in traditional medicine, and are also used in some modern supplements due to their potential benefits in treating various health conditions.
